title: Reading: Decoding Matras and Common Objects
learningGoal: Apply the rules of Matras to read real Hindi words and simple sentences describing objects.
instructions: In this reading exercise, you will practice decoding Matras (vowel signs). The sentences use the 'Yeh ... hai' (This is a ...) structure. Look closely at the vowel signs attached to the consonants to read the vocabulary words.
readingText:
अमित: नमस्ते राहुल। यह क्या है? (What is this?)
राहुल: नमस्ते अमित। यह किताब है।
अमित: और वह क्या है? (And what is that?)
राहुल: वह गाड़ी है। गाड़ी अमेरिका से है।
अमित: क्या यह कंप्यूटर है? (Is this a computer?)
राहुल: हाँ, यह कंप्यूटर है।
अमित: धन्यवाद राहुल।
राहुल: नमस्ते।
textInTargetLanguage:
अमित: नमस्ते राहुल। यह क्या है?
राहुल: नमस्ते अमित। यह किताब है।
अमित: और वह क्या है?
राहुल: वह गाड़ी है। गाड़ी अमेरिका से है。
अमित: क्या यह कंप्यूटर है?
राहुल: हाँ, यह कंप्यूटर है।
अमित: धन्यवाद राहुल।
राहुल: नमस्ते।
baseLanguageSupportText:
Amit: Hello Rahul. What is this?
Rahul: Hello Amit. This is a book.
Amit: And what is that?
Rahul: That is a car. The car is from America.
Amit: Is this a computer?
Rahul: Yes, this is a computer.
Amit: Thank you Rahul.
Rahul: Goodbye.

options: Woh gaadi hai. (That is a car.)

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: What is the Hindi word for 'Book' in the text?
Look at the word 'क्या' (Kya). What do you notice about the first letter 'k'?
options: It is a half-letter 'k' (क्) joined to 'ya' (या).
It has an 'aa' matra on it.
It has a short 'i' matra on it.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Grammar Check: When the word 'Kya' (क्या) is placed at the VERY BEGINNING of a sentence ('Kya yeh kampyutar hai?'), what happens to the sentence?
options: It turns the sentence into a Yes/No question (Is this a computer?).

correctAnswers.rq_5: It is a half-letter 'k' (क्) joined to 'ya' (या).
correctAnswers.rq_6: It turns the sentence into a Yes/No question (Is this a computer?).

expectedSummaryPoints: Identify words with matras like Kitaab and Gaadi.
Understand the difference between Yeh (this) and Woh (that).
Recognize the Yes/No question structure using Kya.

feedbackRules.onPass: Superb reading! You successfully decoded matras and half-letters in context.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the Matras lesson. Remember that 'ki' in kitaab has a short 'i' matra that goes before the letter.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Note that placing 'Kya' at the start of a sentence simply makes it a Yes/No question.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: अमित: नमस्ते राहुल। यह क्या है? राहुल: नमस्ते अमित। यह किताब है। अमित: और वह क्या है? राहुल: वह गाड़ी है। गाड़ी अमेरिका से है。 अमित: क्या यह कंप्यूटर है? राहुल: हाँ, यह कंप्यूटर है। अमित: धन्यवाद राहुल। राहुल: नमस्ते।

hintText: Compare 'Yeh kya hai?' (What is this) with 'Kya yeh kitaab hai?' (Is this a book). The position of 'kya' changes its meaning.
commonMistakes: Reading 'kya' as 'ka-ya' instead of a blended 'kya'.
